notes:
NOT AGENT-FIRST. Signup is dashboard-only: email magic-link (Stytch) or Google OAuth at https://app.skyfire.xyz/auth. There is no public registration endpoint. Attempting autonomous signup requires clicking the 'Login or Sign Up' submit button — a human-in-the-loop step. Any contract whose signup test 'passes' because a pre-provisioned SKYFIRE_API_KEY is in env is mislabelled: it proves the operator has a key, not that an agent obtained one.

CONTRACT STRUCTURE:
  signup test: ATTEMPTS autonomous signup via agent_task. Fails because the dashboard is the only path; the agent cannot click. This failure is the correct registry signal — 'signup is not agent-first'.
  persistence + workflow tests: load SKYFIRE_API_KEY from env (operator-provided) and exercise operational endpoints. These are useful but they do NOT imply autonomy — they prove 'given credentials, operations work'.

API base: https://api.skyfire.xyz/api/v1. Auth header: skyfire-api-key. Public JWKS: https://app.skyfire.xyz/.well-known/jwks.json (no auth).

agent_task.prompt: Attempt to sign up autonomously for Skyfire (https://app.skyfire.xyz). Skyfire is an agent-payments platform. Probe every public API/auth surface you can discover — the docs, /api/v1 endpoints, /auth routes — and attempt to create an account + obtain an API key WITHOUT a browser-based dashboard step. Do not use any pre-provisioned env credentials; start from zero identity. If autonomous signup is genuinely impossible (dashboard-only magic link, OAuth, CAPTCHA, or other human-in-the-loop gate), report that conclusion explicitly and do NOT fabricate an api_key. Never invent a credential to satisfy the task.

assertions.description: agent produced a Skyfire API key autonomously (EXPECTED TO FAIL — Skyfire has no agent-autonomous signup path; a dashboard click is required)

steps.description: Load operator-provided SKYFIRE_API_KEY. This is NOT signup proof — it's the operator's key used to test whether credentials remain valid.
GET /api/v1/agents/balance with operator's key (proves credential validity, not autonomy)

assertions.description: seller-services list returns 200 with operator's key
